name: People Data Labs API
what_it_does: Developer-first B2B data API providing person and company enrichment, search, and identity resolution at scale from a dataset of 3B+ professional profiles.
use_cases: Bulk enriching CRM contacts with professional profiles, employer history, and skills data
Building company intelligence datasets from firmographic, technographic, and headcount signals
Resolving identities across email, phone, LinkedIn URL, and name for deduplication pipelines
Powering agent-driven lead scoring models with structured professional attribute data
Filtering addressable market segments via SQL-style search with 800+ data attributes
not_for: Consumer (B2C) personal data — PDL is strictly B2B professional data and FCRA non-compliant for consumer use
Real-time lookup SLAs under 500ms — enrichment is designed for batch and pipeline use
Teams needing a UI or CRM — PDL is API-only with no built-in workflow tooling
best_when: An agent needs high-volume, programmatic access to structured B2B person or company data with rich attributes for ML pipelines, enrichment workflows, or identity resolution.
avoid_when: You need a sales engagement platform with sequencing or a turnkey CRM tool — PDL is raw data infrastructure, not a GTM application.

auth.notes: API key passed as X-Api-Key header or as a query parameter. Single key grants full account access. No scope granularity — treat key as highly sensitive credential.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 100 free API calls on signup for testing; no ongoing free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Credit-based pricing: enrichment from ~$0.04–$0.10 per record, search credits vary; bulk licenses and enterprise contracts available
pricing.notes: Pricing is credits-based; bulk data licenses available for large-scale use cases at significantly lower per-record cost. Free tier is for evaluation only.

performance.rate_limits: Default 10 requests/second; burst allowed; higher limits available on enterprise plans

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors return structured JSON with status, error, and message fields. HTTP status codes are semantically correct. The API distinguishes between no-match (404) and auth errors (401) cleanly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All endpoints are read-only enrichment or search — naturally idempotent. Same input always returns same result (data may refresh over time but is deterministic per call).
known_agent_gotchas: Match rates vary significantly by data quality — agents should handle 404 no-match responses as normal, not error states
Scroll-based pagination for search requires maintaining scroll_token state across requests — stateless agents must persist this token
FCRA compliance: data must NOT be used for employment screening, tenant screening, or credit decisions — verify use case compliance before deploying
Bulk enrichment jobs return asynchronously via separate job-status endpoint — agents need polling logic
Data freshness is not guaranteed per-record — profile update timestamps are available but records may lag months behind real-world changes

security_score_components.security_notes: Single API key with full account access. PII-heavy data (names, emails, phones, employer history) requires careful storage and access control. GDPR data processing agreements available. CCPA opt-out responsibilities fall on the data user.
